name: Baked Beans (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, leaving enough extra wrap to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans if food will be served without freezing.
2. Add beans to boiling water; return to boil. Boil beans 2 minutes, then soak beans for 1 hour (or overnight).
3. Add salt pork and salt to beans. Cook beans slowly until tender, about 1 1/2 hours. Drain; save 2 cups cooking liquid.
4. Mix bean cooking liquid, molasses, brown sugar, mustard, and onion in a bowl.
5. Pour the mixture over the beans. Mix gently.
6. Pour one-fourth of the mixture into each baking dish.
To Serve Without Freezing:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (moderate).
2. Bake for 1 hour or until beans are lightly browned and sauce is desired consistency.
To Freeze:
1. Cool dishes for 30 minutes at room temperature.
2. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over the top of the food.
3. Put edges of wrap together and fold several times so paper lies directly on top of the food.
4. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape.
5. Label with name of food, date frozen, and last date for best eating quality (about 6 months).
6. Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from pans.
To Heat Frozen Food:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (moderate).
2. Remove freezer wrap.
3. Place food in a baking pan.
4. Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until center is hot.
